author: claude
text:
task types: free-form identifier labels

Replace the closed TaskType enum (Epic/Task/Bug/Unknown) with a
newtype wrapping String. `bl create -t` now accepts any identifier
matching [a-z][a-z0-9_-]*, so feature/chore/spike/question/etc.
work out of the box. Only `epic` still drives behavior (progress
bar, [epic] marker) — every other value is a pure label.

Deserialize stays lenient: a future bl writing an arbitrary label
(including one `parse` would reject) round-trips through an older
bl unchanged. Identifier enforcement is a create-time rule, not a
load-time rule.

Scoped down from the original task: no config-declared vocabulary
and no strict-mode flag. Those are additive polish if demand
shows up; the value of this change is letting `-t spike` work
today. Common vocabulary surfaces in `-t` help text as
suggestions; the SUGGESTIONS constant doubles as self-test input.
